title: Detecting Non-Nil Tail in a Singly Linked List

statement.markdown: Given a singly linked list, write a function to detect whether it has a non-nil tail. Note that a linked list has a non-nil tail if it contains at least one node after its head node. You should handle the case where there is only one node in the linked list.
statement.inputFormat: A singly linked list is represented as a list of integers where each integer is a node value.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the linked list is non-nil.
statement.notes: You can assume that the linked list nodes are well-formed with correct next pointers.

examples.explanation: The linked list has at least one node after its head node.
The linked list has no node after its head node.

buggyStarterCode.python:
class Solution:
    def hasNonNilTail(self, head: list[int]) -> bool:
        if head is None: return False
        while head.next is not None: head = head.next
        return head.next is not None
        # BUG: Incorrectly checking for None before checking for next pointer
        return False
